source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A simple harmonic transverse wave propagates along the positive direction of the $x$-axis. The equilibrium position of the wave source is at the origin of the coordinate system. The vibration graph of the wave source within $0 \sim 4s$ is shown in the figure. It is known that the wave propagation speed is $0.5\mathsf{m}/\mathsf{s}$.

1. Determine the wavelength of this transverse wave.
2. Determine the distance traveled by the wave source within 4s.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the vibration graph is shown in the figure
image_manifest contains 1 existing image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: part 1 requires combining graph-derived temporal information with the given wave speed to determine wavelength
part 2 requires extracting motion information of the source from the graph rather than directly reading a single value
answer_text contains two separate numerical results, consistent with multi-part reasoning
verifiability: answer_text gives explicit target results with units: wavelength and distance
final answers are numeric physics quantities that are checkable against the graph and given speed

risk_flags: graph-derived values are not fully specified in text and depend on correct image interpretation
short_text answer may have formatting or unit-expression variation

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required image are all present, and asset registry passed with no reported issues
image dependency is very high, but the needed graph image exists, so the core semantics are recoverable
multi-step and verifiability scores are strong, indicating the sample is useful and checkable
risk flags are manageable and do not indicate missing core semantic support
